item.title: Page - Turn your computer into a home server
item.description: GitHub: Page-Android page-python Summary Clean UI Built on Android with Java Leverages Architecture Components: Room: Storage of defined models with the ability to serve live data. This keeps the application reactive and consistent without the need to manually update UI components when data changes. LiveData: The above-mentioned realtime nature of the application is made possible mainly because of LiveData in conjunction with Room. LiveData keeps datastreams up-to-date and allows propagation of changes through multiple layers.

item.title: Converting Intricate Recursive Functions to Iterative Code
item.description: Recursive functions are pretty awesome. They allow programmers to solve problems that are naturally visualized as self-repeating or branching, and for this reason they’re also easier to understand compared to iterative code. So why give up all this goodness and even think about anything else? Pros of iteration over recursion Overhead of functions (recursive or otherwise): Functions are super helpful in that they encapsulate code and let us call them as many times as we like and they’ll do exactly what we tell them.

item.title: CLRS Exercise 6.1.1 Solution
item.description: Question: What are the minimum and maximum numbers of elements in a heap of height ‘h’? Let’s start by defining what a heap is. A heap is a data structure that can be viewed as an almost complete binary tree. What’s an almost complete binary tree? It’s basically a complete binary tree where the last level is not full. This may seem confusing at first as that definition also fits the definition of a complete binary tree, but the main difference lies in that the almost complete binary tree is not filled at the last level.

item.title: About
item.description: I’m Krishna. I’m a Java and Android developer. I also work with computer networks, Linux programming, and a few open source projects. I live in Bangalore (India) and I have a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science and Engineering! Hit me up, tell me what’s up with you.

item.title: Herl - An application to create short links with dynamic destinations
item.description: Live At herl.me Summary Backend built with Go and MongoDB Frontend built with HTML, CSS, JS and Bootstrap Authentication and analytics built with Firebase SSL/TLS certificate provided by Let’s Encrypt Hosted on Google Cloud Platform’s Compute Engine offering on the basic instance. Goroutines allow concurrent handling of large numbers of client requests simultaneously. This coupled with Go’s speed result in a monster of a server. Mongo’s interface to Go allow easy interaction with the database.

item.title: Converting Videos to Frames in OpenCV
item.description: Here’s how you can read a video file in OpenCV and convert it to individual frames: import cv2 as cv import numpy as np # open reference to video file videoReference = cv.VideoCapture('path_to_video') # for example 'video.mp4' or '~/Downloads/video.mp4' # create an ndarray for storing frames frames = [] read_result = videoReference.read() # the read() method returns a tuple like (frame_exists, frame) while read_result[0]: frames.append(read_result[1]) read_result = videoReference.read() frames = np.
